_note: Original Practice questions aligned with CS Navigator's original Learn lesson for this topic.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 1
questions.prompt: Which modifier limits a field to its own class?
questions.explanation: A private member can be accessed only inside the class that declares it. Callers must use the public operations the class provides.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 2
questions.prompt: Which line imports ArrayList from its package?
questions.explanation: Java import statements begin with `import` and use the full package and class name. The statement ends with a semicolon.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers summary
questions.prompt: Which statement best describes Packages & Access Modifiers?
choices: Organize classes and control which code may use their details.
It is used only to print a message.
It automatically fixes every program error.
It removes the need for variables and tests.
questions.explanation: A package groups related classes and gives them a shared name. Put the package statement first in the file. Use `public` for code meant for callers, `private` for class details, and default access for code shared only inside the package.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers practice 5
questions.prompt: What does making `credits` private and exposing `addCredits` prevent?
choices: Outside code setting credits directly to an invalid value like -5
The class from being used in other packages
The credits field from ever changing
questions.explanation: A private field cannot be written from outside, so no caller can do `obj.credits = -5`. Changes must go through `addCredits`, which rejects a non-positive amount. The field still changes through that method, and the class is still usable elsewhere and still constructible.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers concept check 1
questions.prompt: What does the lesson mean by "Folders and package names must agree"?
choices: If a file declares `package school.records`, keep it under the matching `school/records` path for normal builds. A mismatch often leads to class loading or import errors.
questions.explanation: A package groups related classes and gives them a shared name. Put the package statement first in the file. That reasoning supports the answer: If a file declares `package school.records`, keep it under the matching `school/records` path for normal builds. A mismatch often leads to class loading or import errors.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers concept check 2
questions.prompt: Which choice describes a reliable Packages & Access Modifiers practice?
choices: Group classes that solve related problems.
questions.explanation: A package groups related classes and gives them a shared name. Put the package statement first in the file. That reasoning supports the answer: Group classes that solve related problems.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers concept check 3
questions.prompt: Which recommendation belongs in a review of Packages & Access Modifiers code?
choices: Match package names to the directory structure.
questions.explanation: A package groups related classes and gives them a shared name. Put the package statement first in the file. That reasoning supports the answer: Match package names to the directory structure.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers concept check 4
questions.prompt: Which guideline helps prevent a common Packages & Access Modifiers mistake?
choices: Keep fields private unless outside access is truly required.
questions.explanation: A package groups related classes and gives them a shared name. Put the package statement first in the file. That reasoning supports the answer: Keep fields private unless outside access is truly required.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers concept check 5
questions.prompt: A classmate is checking their understanding of Packages & Access Modifiers. Which advice is correct?
choices: Expose small public methods with clear jobs.
questions.explanation: A package groups related classes and gives them a shared name. Put the package statement first in the file. That reasoning supports the answer: Expose small public methods with clear jobs.

questions.title: Packages & Access Modifiers concept check 6
questions.prompt: Which choice shows sound reasoning about Packages & Access Modifiers?
choices: Import classes instead of repeating long names throughout a file.
questions.explanation: A package groups related classes and gives them a shared name. Put the package statement first in the file. That reasoning supports the answer: Import classes instead of repeating long names throughout a file.
